zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry le Scrope於1271年出生在英國越可爾布拉可維爾時，他的父親William le Scrope Lord of Bolton11268歲，母親Constance Newsom11268歲。他和Margaret FitzWalter至少有3個兒子。他於1330年被列為english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.。他去世於1336年9月7日享年65歲，埋葬在英國越可爾里士滿。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry le Scrope于1271年出生在英国越可爾布拉可維爾时，他的父亲William le Scrope Lord of Bolton11268岁，母亲Constance Newsom11268岁。他和Margaret FitzWalter至少有3个儿子。他于1330年被列为english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.。他去世于1336年9月7日享年65岁，埋葬在英国越可爾里士滿。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry le Scrope was born in 1271, in Bracewell, Yorkshire, England, his father, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, was 11268 and his mother, Constance Newsom, was 11268. He had at least 3 sons with Margaret FitzWalter. In 1330, at the age of 59, his occupation is listed as english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.. He died on 7 September 1336, at the age of 65, and was buried in Richmond, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry le Scrope est né en 1271, à Bracewell, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, avait 11268 ans et sa mère, Constance Newsom, avait 11268 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Margaret FitzWalter. En 1330, à l’âge de 59 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.. Il est décédé le 7 septembre 1336, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Richmond, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry le Scrope 1271 in Bracewell, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William le Scrope Lord of Bolton 11268 und seine Mutter Constance Newsom 11268. Er hatte mit Margaret FitzWalter mindestens 3 Söhne. 1330, als er 59 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. aufgeführt. Er starb am 7. September 1336 im Alter von 65 Jahren und wurde in Richmond, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry le Scrope è nato il nel 1271 nel Bracewell, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, aveva 11268 anni e il suo madre, Constance Newsom, aveva 11268 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Margaret FitzWalter. Nel 1330, all'età di 59 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.. Morì il 7 settembre 1336, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Richmond, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry le Scropeが1271年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブレイセウエルで生まれた時，父William le Scrope Lord of Boltonは11268歳，母Constance Newsomは11268歳であった。彼はMargaret FitzWalterとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。1330年，59歳の時，職業はenglish lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.と記録されている。彼は1336年9月7日に65歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアリチモンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry le Scrope(이)가 1271년 영국 요르크실르 브라스웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William le Scrope Lord of Bolton(은)는 11268세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance Newsom(은)는 11268세였다. 그는 Margaret FitzWalter(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1330년, 59세의 나이로 그의 직업은 english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.이었다. 그는 1336년 9월 7일 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 리ch몽에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry le Scrope nasceu em 1271, em Bracewell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, tinha 11268 anos e sua mãe, Constance Newsom, tinha 11268 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Margaret FitzWalter. Em 1330, com 59 anos, sua profissão aparece como english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.. Ele faleceu em 7 de setembro de 1336, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Richmond, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry le Scrope родился в 1271 г. в местности бреисюелл, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William le Scrope Lord of Bolton было 11268 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance Newsom, 11268. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Margaret FitzWalter. В 1330 году, в возрасте 59 лет,  он работал по профессии english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. Он умер 7 сентября 1336 г. в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности ричмонд, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry le Scrope nació en 1271, en Bracewell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, tenía 11268 años y su mamá, Constance Newsom, tenía 11268 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Margaret FitzWalter. En 1330, a la edad de 59 años, su ocupación registrada era english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench.. Murió el 7 de septiembre de 1336, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Richmond, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
